[[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall {{DEFAULTSORT:Moritano Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická == name in kana (P1814) == * もりたのじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005000847 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Moritano Shrine|lt=Moritano Shrine|WD=Q135039929|1=Moritano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県長野市高田174', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長野県長野市七二会', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * もりたのじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6100005000847 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Moritano Shrine|lt=Moritano Shrine|WD=Q135039929|1=Moritano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県長野市高田174', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長野県長野市七二会', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[JA:守田迺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11450096}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Moritano Shrine |Image = [[File:Moritano-jinja Shrine, Takada, Nagano.jpg|270px|Moritano Shrine]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}} 174 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal enshrined deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} / {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Morita Shrine |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|WD=Q2888|1=September 23}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Moritano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Moritano Shrine''' (もりたのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Takada|1=Takada}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Morita Shrine'''. It is identified with the Morita Shrine listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The shrine is located in a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=residential area|1=UNKNOWN}} in the eastern part of Nagano City, one street removed from the bustle of {{ill|lt=National Route 19|Q899326|WD=Q899326|1=Japan National Route 19}}. Although the date of foundation is unknown, it was formerly called '''Morita Hachimangū''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of the former {{ill|lt=Takada (Nagano City)|Q11672003|WD=Q11672003|lt=Kita-Takada Village|1=Kita-Takada Village}}[http://www.komakichiku.com/siseki/ Our Town’s Historic Sites and Cultural Properties] – Komaki District Residents’自治 Council. In {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), it received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q60523|WD=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. However, during the Shikinaisha naming dispute in {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10), the character “迺 (or 廼)” was added to its name. The main hall was formerly the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}}. When the Year Deity Hall was relocated from the Zenkō-ji precincts to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) following the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, only the building was transferred to this shrine[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/070.html No. 070 Moritano Shrine (Part 2) – The main hall transferred from Zenkō-ji] – Weekly Nagano. Because it was relatively close to {{ill|lt=Tsuruga (Nagano City)#Higashi-Tsuruga Town|Q11676187|WD=Q11676187|lt=Tsuruga Shinchi|1=Tsuruga}} (the former Nagano red-light district) and {{ill|lt=Gondō Town (Nagano City)|Q11542303|WD=Q11542303|lt=Gondō Town|1=Gondōchō}}, many of the donors’ names carved on the {{ill|lt=tamagaiki|Q3078312|WD=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence belong to {{ill|lt=ryōtei|Q2178435|WD=Q2178435|1=Ryōtei}} restaurants and brothels[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2011/04/069.html No. 069 Moritano Shrine (Part 1) – Names of restaurants and pleasure quarters on the fence] – Weekly Nagano. === History === * {{ill|lt=1459|Q6524|WD=Q6524|1=1459}} ({{ill|lt=Chōroku|Q1193524|WD=Q1193524|1=Chōroku}} 3) – Relocated from a nearby site to its present location * {{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 12) – Following a divine oracle, the sacred object was transferred from the Year Deity Hall of {{ill|lt=Zenkō-ji|Q189640|WD=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=1756|Q7632|WD=Q7632|1=1756}} ({{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8) – Received {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} under the name '''Morita Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10) – Entered into a shrine name dispute (Shikinaisha dispute) with shrines of the same name in Furuma (present-day {{ill|lt=Shinanomachi (representative topic)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinanomachi}}) and {{ill|lt=Naganuma (Nagano City)|Q17226618|WD=Q17226618|lt=Naganuma|1=Naganuma}} (now part of {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}); as the priest of this shrine was still young at the time, the result was a renaming to '''Moritano Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12) – The former Year Deity Hall of Zenkō-ji, which had been relocated in the same year to {{ill|lt=Joyama Park (Nagano City)|Q11426101|WD=Q11426101|lt=Joyama|1=Jōyama Park}} as {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha), was transferred and rebuilt as this shrine’s main hall * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) – The main hall was designated a {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (principal deity) * Kotoshironushi * Ōusu no Mikoto * Kuebiko no Mikoto * Takeminakata no Mikoto * Susanoo no Mikoto === Precincts === * Main hall – {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=designated cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1049.html Moritano Shrine Main Hall] – Nagano City Cultural Properties Database. Former main hall of the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall * Worship hall * Norito hall * Auxiliary shrines ** Moritano Inari Shrine ** Traffic Safety Guardian Deity === Access === * From {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Nagano Station|Q374916|WD=Q374916|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|lt=Alpico Kōtsū|Q4735443|WD=Q4735443|1=Alpico Kōtsū}} ({{ill|lt=Kawanakajima Bus|Q11477633|WD=Q11477633|1=Kawanakajima Bus}}) Route '''45''' and get off at “Morita Shrine-mae” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * *Cultural Series of Shinshū: Temples and Shrines*, 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Company|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} === Related items === * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomino Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City, Hakoshimizu)|Q11387218|WD=Q11387218|1=Takeminakata}} (Minochi Taisha) – Formerly the Zenkō-ji Year Deity Hall; the main hall of this shrine was transferred from that hall == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moritano Shrine (Minochi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]
